title: Modules
text: checks can done simple text search, can create module provides arguments search_text() function, along report output.

title: papercheck
text: can install development version papercheck GitHub :

title: papercheck
text: function pdf2grobid() can read PDF files save TEI format created grobid. requires internet connection takes seconds per paper, done results saved later use. server unavailable, can use grobid web interface.
code: pdf_file <- demofiles("pdf")[[2]] xml_file <- pdf2grobid(pdf_file) xml_file <- demofiles("xml")[[2]]

title: papercheck
text: function read_grobid() can read XML files parsed grobid.

title: papercheck
text: can access parsed table full text paper via s$full_text, may find convenient use function search_text(). defaults return data table sentence, section type, header, div, paragraph sentence numbers, file name. (section type best guess headers, may always accurate.)

title: papercheck
text: can search specific word phrase setting pattern argument. pattern regex string default; set fixed = TRUE want find exact text matches.

title: papercheck
text: Set section vector sections search .

title: papercheck
text: Set return one “sentence”, “paragraph”, “section”, “match” control gets returned.

title: papercheck
text: can also return just matched text regex search setting return = "match". extra ... arguments search_text() passed grep(), perl = TRUE allows use complex regex, like .
code: pattern <- "[^\\s\\(\\)]+\\s*(=|<)\\s*[0-9\\.-]+" text <- search_text(paper, pattern, return = "match", perl = TRUE)

title: papercheck
text: functions pdf2grobid() read_grobid() also work folder files, returning list XML file paths scienceverse study objects, respectively. search_text() also works list study objects.
code: grobid_dir <- system.file("grobid", package="papercheck")  papers <- read_grobid(grobid_dir)  hypotheses <- search_text(papers, "hypothesi",                            section = "intro",                            return = "paragraph")

title: papercheck
code: module_list() #>              name                     title type #> 1    ai-summarise        Summarise Sections   ai #> 2    all-p-values         List All P-Values text #> 3        all-urls             List All URLs text #> 4     imprecise-p        Imprecise P-Values code #> 5        marginal     Marginal Significance text #> 6       osf-check Check Status of OSF Links code #> 7 retractionwatch           RetractionWatch code #>                                                                     path #> 1    /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/papercheck/modules/ai-summarise.mod #> 2    /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/papercheck/modules/all-p-values.mod #> 3        /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/papercheck/modules/all-urls.mod #> 4     /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/papercheck/modules/imprecise-p.mod #> 5        /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/papercheck/modules/marginal.mod #> 6       /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/papercheck/modules/osf-check.mod #> 7 /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/papercheck/modules/retractionwatch.mod p <- module_run(papers, "all-p-values")  head(p) #> $module #> [1] "all-p-values" #>  #> $title #> [1] "List All P-Values" #>  #> $table #> # A tibble: 24 × 7 #>    text      section header              div     p     s id           #>  * <chr>     <chr>   <chr>             <dbl> <dbl> <int> <chr>        #>  1 p = 0.012 intro   [div-01]              1     4     6 eyecolor.xml #>  2 p = 0.849 intro   [div-01]              1     4     7 eyecolor.xml #>  3 p = 0.019 intro   [div-01]              1     4     8 eyecolor.xml #>  4 p = 0.879 intro   [div-01]              1     4     9 eyecolor.xml #>  5 p = 0.266 intro   [div-01]              1     4    11 eyecolor.xml #>  6 p = 0.403 intro   [div-01]              1     4    13 eyecolor.xml #>  7 p = 0.13  intro   [div-01]              1     4    14 eyecolor.xml #>  8 p = 0.337 intro   [div-01]              1     4    15 eyecolor.xml #>  9 p = 0.1   intro   [div-01]              1     4    16 eyecolor.xml #> 10 p = 0.029 method  Statistical tests     5     1    13 eyecolor.xml #> # title: papercheck
text: can query extracted text papers chatGPT.

title: papercheck
text: Run need provide API key. can enter manually call gpt(), add following lines .Renviron file user project.
code: gpt_setup() CHATGPT_KEY="sk-proj-abcdefghijklmnopqrs0123456789ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRS" RETICULATE_PYTHON="~/.virtualenvs/r-reticulate/bin/python" # useful if you aren't sure where this file is usethis::edit_r_environ()

title: papercheck
code: query <- "What is the hypothesis of this study?" gpt_hypo <- gpt(hypotheses, query)

title: Authors and Citation
text: Lisa DeBruine. Author, maintainer. Daniel Lakens. Author.

title: Authors and Citation
text: DeBruine L, Lakens D (2024). papercheck: Check Scientific Papers Best Practices. R package version 0.0.0.9014, https://scienceverse.github.io/papercheck/, https://github.com/debruine/papercheck.
code: @Manual{,   title = {papercheck: Check Scientific Papers for Best Practices},   author = {Lisa DeBruine and Daniel Lakens},   year = {2024},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9014, https://scienceverse.github.io/papercheck/},   url = {https://github.com/debruine/papercheck}, }

what: Check Scientific Papers for Best Practices
title: Check Scientific Papers for Best Practices
text: goal papercheck automatically check scientific papers best practices.

title: Check Scientific Papers for Best Practices
text: can install development version papercheck GitHub :

title: Check Scientific Papers for Best Practices
text: Convert PDF grobid XML format, read scienceverse paper object. Search returned text. regex pattern searches text looks like statistical values (e.g., N=313 p = 0.17).
code: library(papercheck) pdf <- demofiles("pdf")[2] grobid <- pdf2grobid(pdf) paper <- read_grobid(grobid) pattern <- "[^\\s\\(\\)]+\\s*(=|<)\\s*[0-9\\.-]+" text <- search_text(paper, pattern,                      return = "match",                      perl = TRUE)

title: Check Scientific Papers for Best Practices
code: # read in all the XML files in the demo directory grobid_dir <- demofiles() papers <- read_grobid(grobid_dir)  # select sentences in the intros containing the text "previous" previous <- search_text(papers, "previous",                          section = "intro",                          return = "sentence") knitr::kable(previous)

title: Check Scientific Papers for Best Practices
text: Papercheck designed modularly, can add modules check anything. comes set pre-defined modules, hope people share modules. can see list built-modules function . run built-module paper, can reference name.
code: module_list() #>              name                     title type #> 1    ai-summarise        Summarise Sections   ai #> 2    all-p-values         List All P-Values text #> 3        all-urls             List All URLs text #> 4     imprecise-p        Imprecise P-Values code #> 5        marginal     Marginal Significance text #> 6       osf-check Check Status of OSF Links code #> 7 retractionwatch           RetractionWatch code #> 8  sample-size-ml               Sample Size   ml #>                                                                                                                                 path #> 1    /private/var/folders/sw/fftq36pn4wj66bj_pjvh5fpw0000gn/T/RtmpiP9OCw/temp_libpath2899724073b/papercheck/modules/ai-summarise.mod #> 2    /private/var/folders/sw/fftq36pn4wj66bj_pjvh5fpw0000gn/T/RtmpiP9OCw/temp_libpath2899724073b/papercheck/modules/all-p-values.mod #> 3        /private/var/folders/sw/fftq36pn4wj66bj_pjvh5fpw0000gn/T/RtmpiP9OCw/temp_libpath2899724073b/papercheck/modules/all-urls.mod #> 4     /private/var/folders/sw/fftq36pn4wj66bj_pjvh5fpw0000gn/T/RtmpiP9OCw/temp_libpath2899724073b/papercheck/modules/imprecise-p.mod #> 5        /private/var/folders/sw/fftq36pn4wj66bj_pjvh5fpw0000gn/T/RtmpiP9OCw/temp_libpath2899724073b/papercheck/modules/marginal.mod #> 6       /private/var/folders/sw/fftq36pn4wj66bj_pjvh5fpw0000gn/T/RtmpiP9OCw/temp_libpath2899724073b/papercheck/modules/osf-check.mod #> 7 /private/var/folders/sw/fftq36pn4wj66bj_pjvh5fpw0000gn/T/RtmpiP9OCw/temp_libpath2899724073b/papercheck/modules/retractionwatch.mod #> 8  /private/var/folders/sw/fftq36pn4wj66bj_pjvh5fpw0000gn/T/RtmpiP9OCw/temp_libpath2899724073b/papercheck/modules/sample-size-ml.mod p <- module_run(papers, "all-p-values")  head(p$table) #> # A tibble: 6 × 7 #>   text      section header     div     p     s id           #>   <chr>     <chr>   <chr>    <dbl> <dbl> <int> <chr>        #> 1 p = 0.012 intro   [div-01]     1     4     6 eyecolor.xml #> 2 p = 0.849 intro   [div-01]     1     4     7 eyecolor.xml #> 3 p = 0.019 intro   [div-01]     1     4     8 eyecolor.xml #> 4 p = 0.879 intro   [div-01]     1     4     9 eyecolor.xml #> 5 p = 0.266 intro   [div-01]     1     4    11 eyecolor.xml #> 6 p = 0.403 intro   [div-01]     1     4    13 eyecolor.xml

title: Check Scientific Papers for Best Practices
code: # ask chatGPT a question query <- "What is the hypothesis of this study?" gpt_hypo <- gpt(hypotheses, query, group_by = "id")

title: Check Scientific Papers for Best Practices
code: statcheck <- stats(papers)  check <- statcheck |>   dplyr::filter(error == TRUE) |>   dplyr::select(id, computed_p:one_tailed_in_txt)

0.196). #> 15                                                                                                                                                                                                                    Our main, omnibus analysis showed a significant three-way interaction among incest type, participant sex, and sibling type (Estimate = 0.285, t(578.003) = 2.359, p = 0.019). #> 16                                                                                                                                                                                                       Our analysis of moral wrongness scores for sibling incest scores showed a significant interaction between participant sex and sibling type (Estimate = -0.559, t(578) = -2.84, p = 0.005). #> 17                                                                                  Women with brothers only tended to view sibling incest more negatively than did women with sisters only (Estimate = 0.179, t(452) = 2.022, p = 0.044) while men with brothers only tended to view sibling incest less negatively than did men with sisters only (Estimate = -0.38, t(126) = -1.933, p = 0.055). #> 18                                                                                  Women with brothers only tended to view sibling incest more negatively than did women with sisters only (Estimate = 0.179, t(452) = 2.022, p = 0.044) while men with brothers only tended to view sibling incest less negatively than did men with sisters only (Estimate = -0.38, t(126) = -1.933, p = 0.055). #> 19                                                                                                                                                                                        In contrast, our analysis of moral wrongness scores for parental incest scores showed no significant interaction between participant sex and sibling type (Estimate = -0.268, t(578) = -1.373, p = 0.17). #> 20                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             The difference between measurements (M = 2.47, SD = 1.71) was large, t(86) = 13.47, p < 0.001, Hedges' g z = 1.43, 95% CI [1.13;1.73], see Figure 1. #> 21                                                                                                                                                                                                                                The difference between measurements (M = 2.35, SD = 1.69) was analyzed with a dependent t-test, t(85) = 12.87, p < 0.001, Hedges' g z = 1.38, 95% CI [1.08;1.67]. #> 22 Four Welch's independent t-tests using the TOST approach indicated statistical equivalence for the difference between conditions for the perceived benefits, t(311.65) = -3.45, p < 0.001, costs, t(288.61) = -2.26, p = 0.012, how likely researchers were to pre-register, t(323.68) = -4.55, p < 0.001, and percentage of studies they planned to pre-register, t(305.34) = -2.90, p = 0.002. #> 23 Four Welch's independent t-tests using the TOST approach
